faqs.question: What is GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)?
faqs.answer: GEO is the practice of optimizing your website so that AI-powered search engines — including ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Claude, and Gemini — can discover, understand, and cite your content. Unlike traditional SEO, which optimizes for ranked lists of blue links, GEO optimizes for being selected as a cited source in AI-generated answers. The core pillars are: (1) AI crawler access (robots.txt allowing GPTBot, ClaudeBot, etc.), (2) machine-readable content (llms.txt, JSON-LD schema), and (3) authority signals (E-E-A-T).

faqs.question: How is GEO different from SEO?
faqs.answer: SEO optimizes for ranked lists of blue links on search engines like Google and Bing. GEO optimizes for being cited as a source in AI-generated answers. They overlap heavily — good content, structured data, and authority help both — but GEO requires explicit AI-crawler allowance in robots.txt, an llms.txt file, and machine-parseable schema for AI models. In practice, GEO is a superset of SEO that adds AI-specific requirements.

faqs.question: What is llms.txt?
faqs.answer: llms.txt is a markdown file placed at the root of your website (yoursite.com/llms.txt) that provides AI models with a curated summary of your site: what it is, who it serves, and which pages matter most. It is the machine-readable equivalent of an About page, designed to be ingested by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and other LLM-powered crawlers in a single fetch. It is a proposed standard (llmstxt.org) that emerged in 2024-2025.

faqs.question: How does GeoScore work?
faqs.answer: GeoScore audits any public URL across 12 dimensions of GEO readiness (AI crawlability, llms.txt presence, structured data, meta tags, content quality, E-E-A-T signals, brand entity, citation readiness, discovery, agent-friendliness, AI guidance markers, and freshness), producing a 0-100 score plus a prioritized list of fixes. The audit runs entirely in your browser; your URLs are not stored on any server. The source code is MIT-licensed and available on GitHub.

faqs.question: Is GeoScore free?
faqs.answer: Yes. GeoScore is 100% free and open-source under the MIT License. There is no signup, no paywall, and no per-audit limit. The source code is available on GitHub at github.com/qq136692547-cmyk/geo-score. You can also self-host the entire tool on your own domain.

faqs.question: Which AI crawlers should I allow in robots.txt?
faqs.answer: At minimum, allow: GPTBot and OAI-SearchBot (OpenAI/ChatGPT), ClaudeBot and anthropic-ai (Anthropic/Claude), PerplexityBot (Perplexity), Google-Extended (Google Gemini and AI Overviews), and CCBot (Common Crawl, used by many models). GeoScore will tell you exactly which crawlers you are currently blocking and provide a copy-pasteable robots.txt snippet.

faqs.question: What is E-E-A-T and why does it matter for GEO?
faqs.answer: E-E-A-T stands for Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness. Originally a Google Search Quality Rater guideline, it has become a key signal for AI models evaluating whether to cite a source. E-E-A-T is signaled by: author bylines with credentials, About/team pages, third-party reviews and citations, .edu/.gov inbound links, HTTPS, and transparent contact/ownership information. AI models prefer to cite sources that demonstrate real expertise.

faqs.question: Which AI search engines should I optimize for?
faqs.answer: As of 2026, the major AI search engines to optimize for are: ChatGPT (SearchGPT) with ~180M weekly users, Perplexity with ~15M MAU, Google AI Overviews with ~1.5B queries per day, Claude (anthropic-ai bot), Microsoft Copilot, and Gemini. Each has its own crawler (GPTBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, anthropic-ai, etc.) and slightly different content preferences. GeoScore checks all of them.
